summary: Joined AlternativeDVD.com in April 2009 after moving to the San Diego area for a relationship — not the outcome I'd hoped for, but it opened the door to one of the most unique roles of my early career. The company sold video games, consoles, accessories, and gaming merchandise, products I was already passionate about personally, and as a small business everyone wore multiple hats. As the Web Support and E-Commerce Representative, I maintained the online storefront (pricing, product images, catalog changes), managed eBay listings, handled phone-based customer support, and provided technical support for consoles and accessories. This was my first real exposure to the operational side of e-commerce — how online catalogs are maintained, how pricing decisions are managed, and how content changes ripple through to the customer experience. I also worked fully remote from home, years before remote work was common, which demanded real self-discipline, time management, and accountability without day-to-day supervision. Looking back, this role expanded my perspective beyond customer support into web operations and digital content management, and taught me that understanding the business side of technology matters just as much as the technology itself — a lesson that carried directly into enterprise IT. I left in July 2010 to move back to Los Angeles, where my family lives.
highlights: Maintained the AlternativeDVD.com online storefront — updating pricing, product images, and catalog listings to reflect daily market changes — and managed eBay listings alongside phone-based customer support.
Provided technical support for video game consoles and accessories, combining genuine product passion with hands-on troubleshooting.

Got a first real look at the operational side of e-commerce: how online catalogs are maintained, how pricing ripples through to customer experience, and how many moving pieces a small business has to coordinate.
Worked fully remote from home years before remote work was common, building the self-discipline, time management, and accountability needed to manage a workload without direct day-to-day supervision.
Left in July 2010 to move back to Los Angeles for family, carrying forward a new appreciation for how technology, business, and customer experience intersect.
